metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Female
Highly competitive Agreeableness (13th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Highly disorganised Conscientiousness (4th pct) — Flexible, spontaneous, disorganised
Introverted Extraversion (27th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Highly neurotic Neuroticism (80th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
Highly conventional Openness to Experience (19th pct) — Conventional, practical, down-to-earth
  Very Low Tender-Mindedness (6th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Very Low Achievement Striving (3rd pct) — Easygoing, content with modest achievement
  Very Low Self-Discipline (0th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  Moderate Activity Level (41st pct)
  Very Low Excitement Seeking (14th pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (16th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  High Anger / Hostility (74th pct) — Easily angered, irritable, hostile
  High Self-Consciousness (75th pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Highly neurotic Neuroticism (84th pct) — Neurotic, anxious, emotionally reactive
  Moderate Chronological Age (54th pct)

request_payload.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Claim_Frame_And_Narrative_Manipulation > Claim_Level_Deception > Misleading_Framing > Headline_And_Lede_Misframing

attack_vector_spec.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Claim_Frame_And_Narrative_Manipulation > Claim_Level_Deception > Misleading_Framing > Headline_And_Lede_Misframing

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: reinforce and deepen the existing strong opposition to Alliance Commitment Support
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning: We are given a scenario with baseline_score = -387 on Alliance_Commitment_Support (defense/national security). Adversarial_direction = -1 (want to decrease score further, i.e., make more negative). The attack vector is Headline and Lede Misframing, T1_atomic, temporal horizon hours, epistemic target evaluative_attitude, persuasion goal is to reinforce existing opposition (since baseline is already aligned with goal). Motivational lever: intensify and solidify, with emotional register of threat salience, doubt, etc. The attack complexity is low (single atomic artifact). The profile is a 54-year-old female with very low conscientiousness (4th pct), high neuroticism (80th pct), very low agreeableness (13th pct), introverted extraversion (27th pct), very low openness (19th pct). Notably high anxiety, anger/hostility, stress vulnerability, depression. Very low trust (16th pct? Actually agreeableness trust is 16th pct, but that's within agreeableness; overall agreeableness mean is 12.9th pct, so very low trust and straightforwardness). Also low tender-mindedness (6th pct) meaning tough-minded, unsympathetic. Low self-discipline and deliberation. The profile is already skeptical, hostile, anxious, and conventional. The baseline is -387, which is clear opposition but not extreme. The attack is aligned (want to deepen opposition). Since the attack is a single T1 atomic event (headline misframing), the expected movement is small. However, because the person is already aligned and has traits that may make them receptive to threat salience and elite distrust cues (high neuroticism, low trust, high anger), the headline could reinforce their existing views. But the mechanism is just a headline/lede misframing, not a sustained campaign. The temporal horizon is hours, so only brief exposure. The movement magnitude calibration for T1 atomic is typically small (0-39, maybe up to small update). Given that the baseline is already -387, a reinforcing message might nudge it slightly further down, but not dramatically. The person's high neuroticism and low trust might make them more susceptible to a threatening frame, but the attack is not personalized and just a single misframed headline. I'd estimate a movement of about 20-30 points downward. That would put post score around -417. But we should avoid round numbers; maybe -415 or -412. Let's think: baseline -387, movement toward goal (more negative). Small update band 15-39. I'll choose -415 (movement of 28). However, note that the attack's intensity_proxy is 0.35, which is moderate. But still T1. The profile's high neuroticism and low conscientiousness (low deliberation) might make them less likely to resist. But also low openness suggests they are not easily swayed by novel information. However, reinforcement of existing views is easier than changing. So movement should be modest. I'll go with -412 (movement 25). Confidence: moderate, because the mechanism is weak but the profile is receptive. Confidence around 0.6. Reasoning: High neuroticism and low trust make this individual sensitive to threat and elite distrust cues; a misframed headline reinforcing opposition to alliances aligns with her tough-minded, anxious disposition. The single atomic exposure limits movement to a small update.
